reasoning: CTSH has seen a very strong run-up from the mid-60s in October to a high of 84.98 on December 19, driven by several minor contract and IT service announcements. However, a sharp jump on the final day leaves the stock potentially overextended in the short term. The statistical mean-reversion linear model estimates a modest pullback to ~84.33 by next week, generating a P(>84.98) around 0.41-0.49. I assign a 0.44 probability for the first date due to near-term exhaustion and mean reversion. Per instructions, uncertainty increases over longer horizons, bringing distant probabilities closer to 0.5. As time goes on, general market upward drift will likely offset some of the short-term overextension.
